name: OpenAI Codex non-interactive mode
findings: `codex exec` is the documented non-interactive automation command.
A prompt argument plus piped stdin treats stdin as additional context at process start.
`--json` makes stdout a JSONL event stream; it does not document a live stdin turn channel.

name: OpenAI Codex app-server
findings: The app-server protocol is bidirectional JSON-RPC over stdio, websocket, or Unix socket.
Clients start a thread and a turn, then read streamed notifications.
`turn/steer` appends user input to an in-flight turn and is the candidate protocol for Codex live issue/PR feedback.

name: OpenAI Codex CLI reference
findings: The CLI reference documents global flags and the app-server remote mode.
It is the official source for current Codex CLI command surfaces.

name: Claude Code Agent SDK streaming input
findings: Streaming input mode is described as a persistent interactive session.
It supports queued messages, real-time feedback, and context persistence across turns.

name: link-assistant/agent js-v0.24.1 release
findings: The release adds explicit live stream-json idle events.
It documents resume, replay, and interrupt semantics for the Agent CLI live stream-json contract.

name: link-assistant/agent PR #274
findings: The merged PR maps Agent session_idle output to Claude-compatible idle events.
It documents stdin frames, resume/no-fork behavior, replay acknowledgements, interrupts, queueing, and idle boundaries.

name: Local Codex app-server schema probe
findings: The local CLI version checked during research was `codex-cli 0.142.5`.
